case_name: Plan v El-Amir & Anor [2020] EWHC 2902 (QB) (02 November 2020)

evidence_index.text: The claimant's claim of negligence against the first defendant, Mr Ahmed El-Amir, and the second defendant the London Eye Hospital Limited (LEH) in respect of private surgery performed to the claimant's right eye by the first defendant at the second defendant hospital on 23 August 2013
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The claimant was no longer pursuing a claim for breach of a contractual duty of care. Breach of the tortious duty, causation and quantum were all in dispute.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The 9 particular allegations of negligence that were pursued at trial fell into two distinct strands: firstly a failure properly or adequately to obtain the informed consent of the claimant for the surgery and to perform the surgery without such consent
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The expert evidence came from consultant ophthalmic surgeons Mr Robert Morris, MRCP FRCS FRCOphth for the claimant and Professor Claude Claoué, MD, DO, FRCS(Eng) FRCOphth for the first defendant.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The claimant developed glaucoma in her right eye as a result of the surgery from 25 September to 11 December 2013, which she would not have done if the surgery had not been performed.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: He recommended treatment with eye drops and systemic medication such as Diamox as a temporising measure. If control was not achieved by this means, then YAG laser iridotomy/surgical iridectomy also referred to as peripheral iridotomy (PI) should be performed.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Abu-Bakra

evidence_index.text: I find as a matter of fact, that it was negligent for the first defendant not to have urgently performed a PI on the right eye to attempt to relieve the right eye pressure within 48 hours of the appointment on 6 November 2013.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Liability and causation are therefore established against the first defendant in relation to consent.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The first defendant failed to provide the claimant with advice, care and surgical treatment to the reasonable standard expected which caused the glaucoma damage to her right eye
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

patient_demographics.relevant_social_history: Retired psychiatric nurse, independent and energetic

initial_state.chief_complaint: Desire to improve vision to read magazines
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient with age-related macular degeneration (AMD) seeking surgery to improve vision.

focused_exam.eyes: Right eye visual acuity 2/60, left eye hand movements only

requestables.name: Initial Consultation with Dr Qureshi
requestables.description: Consultation regarding potential surgery for AMD

reveal.result_summary: Patient interested in surgery to improve vision, advised surgery on both eyes
reveal.clinical_significance: Patient's desire to improve vision for reading
reveal.decision_impact: Led to decision to proceed with surgery

requestables.name: Eye Examination
requestables.description: Examination of eye condition and pressure

reveal.detailed_findings: Imaging performed but findings not detailed in judgment
reveal.decision_impact: Informed decision to proceed with surgery

timeline_phases.name: Initial Consultation
timeline_phases.description: Patient consults for potential eye surgery

decision_points.prompt: What should be discussed during the consent process?

options.description: Discuss risks and limited benefits of surgery
options.clinical_reasoning: Patient must be informed of risks and realistic outcomes

options.description: Proceed with surgery without detailed consent
options.clinical_reasoning: Patient was not fully informed of risks and benefits

actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Patient was not informed of limited benefits and risks

expected_action_court.description: Full disclosure of risks and benefits should have been provided

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Patient was not informed of the limited benefits and significant risks
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Detailed consent process should have been conducted

decision_points.clinical_context: Post-operative care with raised intraocular pressure
decision_points.prompt: What should be the next step in managing raised IOP?

options.clinical_reasoning: Urgent intervention needed to prevent optic nerve damage

options.description: Continue with medication and delay PI
options.clinical_reasoning: Delay led to further optic nerve damage

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant delayed PI, continued with medication

expected_action_court.description: PI should have been performed urgently
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert consensus on urgency of PI

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Delay in PI led to permanent optic nerve damage
explanation.what_should_have_happened: PI should have been performed within 48 hours

patient_outcome.description: Patient left with no perception of light in right eye
patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Permanent vision loss in right eye

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to perform urgent surgery
Causation established - failure led to permanent vision loss

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to perform urgent surgical intervention
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Urgent surgery could have prevented vision loss

factual_timeline.event: Patient developed glaucoma in right eye

treatments.outcome: Ineffective, led to permanent vision loss

complications.complication: Total loss of vision in right eye
Permanent vision loss in right eye
